message:
fix(http): make chunked transfer encoding and header parsing tolerant of bare LF line endings

The HTTP client's chunked transfer decoder and response header parser
strictly required CRLF (\r\n) line endings per RFC 7230, but some servers
and proxies emit bare LF (\n) endings. This caused parse failures and
unexpected EOF errors when interacting with non-conformant servers.

Changes:
- find_headers_end(): accept both \r\n\r\n and \n\n as header terminators
- parse_response_head(): use str::lines() instead of splitting on \r\n,
  which handles both \r\n and \n transparently
- find_crlf(): return (position, length) tuple to track whether the match
  was CRLF (2 bytes) or LF (1 byte), so callers consume the correct amount
- ChunkedState::SizeLine: consume line_end + len (dynamic) instead of
  hardcoded line_end + 2
- ChunkedState::DataCrlf: accept either \r\n or bare \n after chunk data;
  handle partial reads where only 1 byte is available
- ChunkedState::Trailers: accept bare \n as trailer terminator; use
  find_headers_end() instead of find_double_crlf()
- Remove find_double_crlf() (subsumed by find_headers_end)
- Update tests: find_crlf now returns (pos, len) tuples; add
  find_crlf_present_lf and find_crlf_at_start_lf tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
